GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "Discover your own niche fragrance at David Jones", "Fragrance is personal.", "In recent times, the trend toward niche perfumery has grown, alongside the trend towards customisation in all facets of our life. Fragrance is perhaps the most personal element of our wardrobe and now David Jones is committed to helping us choose a scent that suits our personality and style for that perfect finishing touch to our lives. In the search for your forever fragrance, consider what's known as a "niche" fragrance. These are original scents crafted by boutique perfume houses, meaning they're less common and therefore more unique. For instance, David Jones has a wide range of fragrance brands, many of which yo've probably spritzed for yourself–like Gucci or Marc Jacobs–but have you ever tried a perfume by Kilian, Penhaligons, or Memo Paris? Rather than a fragrance with notes that have a mass appeal, niche fragrances use rare, high-quality ingredients and are often created by bespoke perfume houses with a rich history. Given these exceptional fragrances are so exclusive, it can be difficult to track them down. That's why David Jones has launched an online Fragrance Finder, which only takes mere seconds to use but it will open up a world of possibilities when it comes to finding your own niche aroma. Using the two-step quiz, simply select your main note out of the four notes that describe all modern perfumes–floral, oriental, woody or fresh. Then, David Jones will ask you to select a pairing note, for instance, if you select 'woody' as your main note and then 'woody oriental' as a pairing note, you'll be given a list of niche fragrance options that best suits those desires. You can then shop online or jot down the fragrance names and make an in-store visit to explore these scents. Once you discover niche fragrances, you'll understand what makes them so special. Rather than a one-size-fits-all approach, niche fragrance converts often end up building an entire fragrance wardrobe, with different scents to be worn on different occasions or to suit different moods. As they're such high quality and often focus on core ingredients, you can also layer up scents to create your own unique aroma. To get you started, we recommend exploring Kilian, a brand created in 2007 by an heir to a luxury cognac empire, or Memo Paris, another young label that forged its identity around magical destinations and potent raw materials. Even brands you already love often have a niche fragrance range. For instance, Gucci has a line of seven scents known as The Alchemist's Garden, which are blended with rare raw ingredients and flower essences, and designed to be layered for a bespoke fragrance statement. Similarly, Guerlain has its own exclusive niche range, aptly titled The Exclusives Collection–test out some of our favourites like L'Art et la Matiere or Baiser de Russie. Other brands to explore include Creed, Clive Christian, Amouage and of course, Tom Ford, whose own niche fragrances have a serious cult following. There's so much to discover and since fragrance is so personal, why not do the quiz yourself? Head here to find your perfect scent."
